description: Hormone receptor-positive, HER2-negative breast cancer. Endocrine therapy is foundational; CDK4/6 inhibitors and PI3K inhibitors are key adjunctive classes.

interactionRules.name: PIK3CA + ESR1 co-mutation
interactionRules.description: Co-mutation of PIK3CA and ESR1 indicates a likely endocrine-resistant, PI3K-driven phenotype. Endocrine monotherapy confidence is reduced; PI3K inhibitor combinations are strengthened.

effects.reason: ESR1-driven endocrine resistance amplified by PIK3CA activation.
Combination PI3K + endocrine therapy is a recognized strategy in this dual-driver context.

interactionRules.name: RB1 loss blocks CDK4/6 inhibitor benefit
interactionRules.description: CDK4/6 inhibitors require intact RB1 to exert their cell-cycle arrest effect. RB1 loss-of-function predicts CDK4/6 inhibitor resistance.

effects.reason: RB1 loss eliminates the molecular substrate for CDK4/6 inhibitor activity.
